1	name: Junior Developer agent
2	
3	on:
4	  workflow_dispatch:
5	    inputs:
6	      ticket_key:
7	        description: 'Jira ticket key (e.g. KAN-42)'
8	        required: true
9	        type: string
10	      max_turns:
11	        description: 'Override --max-turns (default 60). Use a small value to exercise the stop condition.'
12	        required: false
13	        type: string
14	        default: '60'
15	
16	jobs:
17	  implement:
18	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
19	    timeout-minutes: 90
20	    permissions:
21	      contents: write
22	      pull-requests: write
23	      id-token: write
24	      actions: write
25	    steps:
26	      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
27	        with:
28	          fetch-depth: 0
29	          # PAT with workflows:write so the agent can push branches that
30	          # add or modify .github/workflows/*. Falls back to GITHUB_TOKEN
31	          # when the PAT isn't configured (workflow-file changes will then
32	          # be rejected at push time, by design).
33	          token: ${{ secrets.DEV_PAT || secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
34	
35	      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
36	        with:
37	          node-version: '20'
38	
39	      - name: Install jira CLI
40	        run: bash scripts/install-jira-cli.sh
41	
42	      - name: Put bin/ on PATH
43	        run: echo "$PWD/bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"
44	
45	      - name: Write jira config
46	        env:
47	          JIRA_SERVER: ${{ vars.JIRA_SERVER }}
48	          JIRA_LOGIN: ${{ vars.JIRA_LOGIN }}
49	          JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
50	          JIRA_BOARD_ID: ${{ vars.JIRA_BOARD_ID }}
51	        run: |
52	          set -euo pipefail
53	          : "${JIRA_SERVER:?JIRA_SERVER repo variable is required}"
54	          : "${JIRA_LOGIN:?JIRA_LOGIN repo variable is required}"
55	          : "${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY:?JIRA_PROJECT_KEY repo variable is required}"
56	          mkdir -p .jira
57	          cat > .jira/config.yml <<EOF
58	          installation: Cloud
59	          server: ${JIRA_SERVER}
60	          login: ${JIRA_LOGIN}
61	          project:
62	              key: ${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY}
63	              type: classic
64	          board:
65	              id: ${JIRA_BOARD_ID:-0}
66	          EOF
67	
68	      - name: Install Claude Code CLI
69	        run: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
70	
71	      - name: Configure git user
72	        run: |
73	          git config --global user.name "github-actions[bot]"
74	          git config --global user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
75	
76	      - name: Create feature branch
77	        env:
78	          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
79	        run: |
80	          set -euo pipefail
81	          git fetch --depth=1 origin main
82	          git checkout -B "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" origin/main
83	          # Reset the remote ref so re-runs of the same ticket start
84	          # clean — matches the local `checkout -B` semantic. A prior
85	          # failed run can leave commits on the remote that diverge
86	          # from current main, which would block fast-forward push.
87	          git push --force origin "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}"
88	
89	      - id: prompt
90	        env:
91	          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
92	        run: |
93	          {
94	            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
95	            echo "TICKET_KEY: ${TICKET_KEY}"
96	            echo "DATE: $(date -u +%F)"
97	            echo
98	            cat agents/junior-dev/prompt.md
99	            echo PROMPT_EOF
100	          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
101	
102	      - id: claude
103	        name: Run Claude (headless)
104	        env:
105	          CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
106	          CONTEXT7_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}
107	          JIRA_CONFIG_FILE: ${{ github.workspace }}/.jira/config.yml
108	          JIRA_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.JIRA_API_TOKEN }}
109	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.DEV_PAT || secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
110	          PROMPT: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
111	          MAX_TURNS: ${{ inputs.max_turns }}
112	        run: |
113	          set +e
114	          claude -p "$PROMPT" \
115	            --permission-mode acceptEdits \
116	            --max-turns "$MAX_TURNS" \
117	            --output-format stream-json \
118	            --verbose \
119	            --allowedTools "Bash(jira issue view:*),Bash(bin/jira issue view:*),Bash(git add:*),Bash(git commit:*),Bash(git status:*),Bash(git diff:*),Bash(git log:*),Bash(git push:*),Bash(git rev-parse:*),Bash(git remote:*),Bash(git config --get:*),Bash(git config --list),Bash(git branch:*),Bash(git show:*),Bash(gh pr create:*),Bash(gh pr view:*),Bash(gh auth status),Bash(gh api repos/:*),Bash(npm test:*),Bash(npm run:*),Bash(ls:*),Bash(cat:*),Bash(printenv:*),Bash(env),Read,Glob,Grep,Edit,Write,mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch" \
120	            > stream.jsonl
121	          rc=$?
122	          echo "claude_exit=$rc" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
123	          # Always succeed; downstream steps handle non-zero claude exit.
124	          exit 0
125	
126	      - name: Convert log to JSON array
127	        if: always()
128	        run: |
129	          set -euo pipefail
130	          if [ -s stream.jsonl ]; then
131	            jq -s '.' stream.jsonl > execution.json
132	          else
133	            echo '[]' > execution.json
134	          fi
135	
136	      - name: Open fallback Draft PR if agent didn't
137	        if: always()
138	        env:
139	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.DEV_PAT || secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
140	          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
141	          CLAUDE_EXIT: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.claude_exit }}
142	        run: |
143	          set -euo pipefail
144	
145	          # Skip if the agent already opened a PR for this branch.
146	          if gh pr view "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" --json url >/dev/null 2>&1; then
147	            echo "PR already exists for junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}; skipping fallback."
148	            exit 0
149	          fi
150	
151	          # Need at least one commit beyond origin/main to have something to push.
152	          commits=$(git rev-list --count "origin/main..HEAD" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
153	          if [ "$commits" -eq 0 ]; then
154	            echo "No commits on branch; nothing to push."
155	            exit 0
156	          fi
157	
158	          # Push the branch (no-op if the agent already pushed).
159	          git push -u origin "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" || true
160	
161	          gh pr create --draft \
162	            --base main \
163	            --head "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" \
164	            --title "[WIP] [${TICKET_KEY}] junior-dev exited before opening PR" \
165	            --body "Junior-dev agent run terminated before opening a PR itself (claude exit code ${CLAUDE_EXIT:-unknown}; likely \`--max-turns\` cap or runtime error). Branch holds whatever the agent committed. See \`.github/agent-logs/junior-dev/\` for the run log."
166	
167	      - name: Commit execution log to main
168	        if: always() && hashFiles('execution.json') != ''
169	        env:
170	          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
171	        run: |
172	          set -e
173	          git fetch --depth=1 origin main
174	          git checkout -f -B main origin/main
175	          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
176	          DEST=".github/agent-logs/junior-dev/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json"
177	          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
178	          cp execution.json "$DEST"
179	          git add "$DEST"
180	          git commit -m "agent log: junior-dev run ${RUN_ID}"
181	          git push origin main
182	
183	      - name: Trigger agent improver
184	        if: always() && hashFiles('execution.json') != ''
185	        env:
186	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
187	          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
188	        run: |
189	          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
190	          gh workflow run agent-improver.yml \
191	            -f agent_name=junior-dev \
192	            -f log_path=".github/agent-logs/junior-dev/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json" \
193	            -f log_ref=main

file.content:
name: Junior Developer agent

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      ticket_key:
        description: 'Jira ticket key (e.g. KAN-42)'
        required: true
        type: string
      max_turns:
        description: 'Override --max-turns (default 60). Use a small value to exercise the stop condition.'
        required: false
        type: string
        default: '60'

jobs:
  implement:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 90
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
      id-token: write
      actions: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          # PAT with workflows:write so the agent can push branches that
          # add or modify .github/workflows/*. Falls back to GITHUB_TOKEN
          # when the PAT isn't configured (workflow-file changes will then
          # be rejected at push time, by design).
          token: ${{ secrets.DEV_PAT || secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'

      - name: Install jira CLI
        run: bash scripts/install-jira-cli.sh

      - name: Put bin/ on PATH
        run: echo "$PWD/bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"

      - name: Write jira config
        env:
          JIRA_SERVER: ${{ vars.JIRA_SERVER }}
          JIRA_LOGIN: ${{ vars.JIRA_LOGIN }}
          JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
          JIRA_BOARD_ID: ${{ vars.JIRA_BOARD_ID }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          : "${JIRA_SERVER:?JIRA_SERVER repo variable is required}"
          : "${JIRA_LOGIN:?JIRA_LOGIN repo variable is required}"
          : "${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY:?JIRA_PROJECT_KEY repo variable is required}"
          mkdir -p .jira
          cat > .jira/config.yml <<EOF
          installation: Cloud
          server: ${JIRA_SERVER}
          login: ${JIRA_LOGIN}
          project:
              key: ${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY}
              type: classic
          board:
              id: ${JIRA_BOARD_ID:-0}
          EOF

      - name: Install Claude Code CLI
        run: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code

      - name: Configure git user
        run: |
          git config --global user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config --global user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

      - name: Create feature branch
        env:
          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          git fetch --depth=1 origin main
          git checkout -B "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" origin/main
          # Reset the remote ref so re-runs of the same ticket start
          # clean — matches the local `checkout -B` semantic. A prior
          # failed run can leave commits on the remote that diverge
          # from current main, which would block fast-forward push.
          git push --force origin "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}"

      - id: prompt
        env:
          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
        run: |
          {
            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
            echo "TICKET_KEY: ${TICKET_KEY}"
            echo "DATE: $(date -u +%F)"
            echo
            cat agents/junior-dev/prompt.md
            echo PROMPT_EOF
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - id: claude
        name: Run Claude (headless)
        env:
          CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          CONTEXT7_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}
          JIRA_CONFIG_FILE: ${{ github.workspace }}/.jira/config.yml
          JIRA_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.JIRA_API_TOKEN }}
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.DEV_PAT || secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          PROMPT: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
          MAX_TURNS: ${{ inputs.max_turns }}
        run: |
          set +e
          claude -p "$PROMPT" \
            --permission-mode acceptEdits \
            --max-turns "$MAX_TURNS" \
            --output-format stream-json \
            --verbose \
            --allowedTools "Bash(jira issue view:*),Bash(bin/jira issue view:*),Bash(git add:*),Bash(git commit:*),Bash(git status:*),Bash(git diff:*),Bash(git log:*),Bash(git push:*),Bash(git rev-parse:*),Bash(git remote:*),Bash(git config --get:*),Bash(git config --list),Bash(git branch:*),Bash(git show:*),Bash(gh pr create:*),Bash(gh pr view:*),Bash(gh auth status),Bash(gh api repos/:*),Bash(npm test:*),Bash(npm run:*),Bash(ls:*),Bash(cat:*),Bash(printenv:*),Bash(env),Read,Glob,Grep,Edit,Write,mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch" \
            > stream.jsonl
          rc=$?
          echo "claude_exit=$rc" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          # Always succeed; downstream steps handle non-zero claude exit.
          exit 0

      - name: Convert log to JSON array
        if: always()
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          if [ -s stream.jsonl ]; then
            jq -s '.' stream.jsonl > execution.json
          else
            echo '[]' > execution.json
          fi

      - name: Open fallback Draft PR if agent didn't
        if: always()
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.DEV_PAT || secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
          CLAUDE_EXIT: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.claude_exit }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail

          # Skip if the agent already opened a PR for this branch.
          if gh pr view "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" --json url >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo "PR already exists for junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}; skipping fallback."
            exit 0
          fi

          # Need at least one commit beyond origin/main to have something to push.
          commits=$(git rev-list --count "origin/main..HEAD" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
          if [ "$commits" -eq 0 ]; then
            echo "No commits on branch; nothing to push."
            exit 0
          fi

          # Push the branch (no-op if the agent already pushed).
          git push -u origin "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" || true

          gh pr create --draft \
            --base main \
            --head "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" \
            --title "[WIP] [${TICKET_KEY}] junior-dev exited before opening PR" \
            --body "Junior-dev agent run terminated before opening a PR itself (claude exit code ${CLAUDE_EXIT:-unknown}; likely \`--max-turns\` cap or runtime error). Branch holds whatever the agent committed. See \`.github/agent-logs/junior-dev/\` for the run log."

      - name: Commit execution log to main
        if: always() && hashFiles('execution.json') != ''
        env:
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
        run: |
          set -e
          git fetch --depth=1 origin main
          git checkout -f -B main origin/main
          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
          DEST=".github/agent-logs/junior-dev/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json"
          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
          cp execution.json "$DEST"
          git add "$DEST"
          git commit -m "agent log: junior-dev run ${RUN_ID}"
          git push origin main

      - name: Trigger agent improver
        if: always() && hashFiles('execution.json') != ''
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
        run: |
          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
          gh workflow run agent-improver.yml \
            -f agent_name=junior-dev \
            -f log_path=".github/agent-logs/junior-dev/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json" \
            -f log_ref=main

content.thinking:
This is a simple one-line change that raises the default `max_turns` from 5 to 60 in the `junior-dev.yml` workflow. The PR description explains this clearly: `5` was a leftover test value.

Let me review what to look for:

1. **Correctness bugs** - The change itself is simple: updating a default value from `5` to `60`. The description is also updated to match. This looks correct.

2. **Security issues** - No security concerns with changing a default value for max_turns.

3. **Performance** - 60 turns is a significantly higher limit than 5. 

With the 90-minute timeout in place, 60 turns should comfortably fit within that window for most scenarios. The description comment is also updated to match the new default, which keeps things consistent. Now I should check if there's documentation elsewhere that needs updating.

content.content:
1	# Junior Developer agent
2	
3	Headless implementation agent for "easy" Jira tickets (story points 1–3).
4	Picks up a ticket key, implements the change, pushes a branch, and opens
5	a Draft PR. Fully autonomous — no human in the loop until the PR review.
6	
7	Part of the agentic workflow sketched in [`docs/agentic-workflow.md`](../../docs/agentic-workflow.md).
8	
9	## Files
10	
11	- `prompt.md` — the agent's prompt. Edit this to change behavior.
12	- `../../.github/workflows/junior-dev.yml` — workflow that runs the agent.
13	
14	## How it runs
15	
16	The workflow `junior-dev.yml` triggers on `workflow_dispatch` only.
17	Dispatches come from two places:
18	
19	- **Manual** — `gh workflow run junior-dev.yml -f ticket_key=KAN-42`.
20	- **Automated** — the Development Orchestrator
21	  ([`orchestrator.yml`](../../.github/workflows/orchestrator.yml))
22	  fires it on the Jira "Dev Ready" transition for every ticket with
23	  story points 1–3, then moves the ticket to **In Progress** so the
24	  same transition can't re-fire it.
25	
26	The workflow installs `jira` and the Claude Code CLI, configures git,
27	creates branch `junior-dev/<TICKET_KEY>` from `origin/main`, then hands
28	off to a single `claude -p` invocation. The agent reads the ticket,
29	implements, commits, pushes, and opens the Draft PR itself.
30	
31	## Decisions
32	
33	These are the three implementation decisions KAN-2 asks the agent
34	author to make. Each decision and why:
35	
36	- **Prompt framing — direct prompt, no skill.** `/goal` and `/ralph-loop`
37	  (mentioned in the original sketch) don't exist as skills in this repo,
38	  and the loop-control mechanism we want (`--max-turns`) is a CLI flag,
39	  not skill behavior. A skill would be an empty wrapper.
40	- **Stop condition — `--max-turns 60` + `timeout-minutes: 90`.** Two
41	  bounded mechanisms. `--max-turns` makes the agent fail fast on hard
42	  tickets without burning the full job budget. The job timeout is a
43	  belt-and-suspenders against any agent step that hangs (e.g. a runaway
44	  test invocation). An attempt counter on the ticket would race the
45	  orchestrator and make failures sticky across runs — rejected.
46	- **Branch naming — `junior-dev/<TICKET_KEY>`.** Predictable, unique
47	  per ticket, and namespaced so it's obvious which agent owns it.
48	  Re-dispatching the same ticket force-resets the branch from
49	  `origin/main` (workflow uses `git checkout -B`), so a re-run gets a
50	  clean start.
51	
52	## Runtime split
53	
54	This agent is the first to run **headless `claude -p` directly** rather
55	than through `anthropics/claude-code-action@v1`. The action stays for
56	read/triage agents (jira-ticket, pr-review, agent-improver) where its
57	event-shaped plumbing is useful. The orchestrator now runs as plain
58	bash with no model. For
59	implementation, the action's PR-comment surface is dead weight; running
60	the CLI directly gives full control over `--max-turns`, allowed tools,
61	and exit handling. See the *Design choices* section in
62	[`docs/agentic-workflow.md`](../../docs/agentic-workflow.md).
63	
64	## Manual run
65	
66	```sh
67	gh workflow run junior-dev.yml -f ticket_key=KAN-42
68	```
69	
70	Optional `max_turns` input overrides `--max-turns` (default 60). Use a
71	small value to deliberately exercise the stop condition (see below).
72	
73	## Stop-condition exercise
74	
75	To see the turn cap in action against a real ticket:
76	
77	```sh
78	gh workflow run junior-dev.yml -f ticket_key=KAN-42 -f max_turns=5
79	```
80	
81	Expected outcome:
82	
83	- Agent reads the ticket and starts implementing.
84	- After 5 turns, `claude` exits non-zero on `--max-turns`.
85	- The workflow detects the non-zero exit, still pushes whatever the
86	  agent committed, and the agent's pre-finish push/PR step may not
87	  have run — in which case the workflow opens a fallback `[WIP]` Draft
88	  PR titled `[WIP] [<KEY>] junior-dev hit turn cap` with a body noting
89	  the cap.
90	- Run log is committed under
91	  `.github/agent-logs/junior-dev/<DATE>/<RUN_ID>.json`.
92	- agent-improver workflow is dispatched as usual.
93	
94	Record the run URL here once exercised:
95	
96	> _Stop condition exercised: <run URL TBD — fill in after first invocation>_
97	
98	## Logs
99	
100	Each run's execution log is committed back to `main` at
101	`.github/agent-logs/junior-dev/<YYYY-MM-DD>/<run-id>.json`. Format
102	matches the array shape produced by `claude-code-action` (the workflow
103	runs `jq -s '.'` over the CLI's JSONL stream so the existing
104	agent-improver `jq` queries work unchanged). Browse with
105	`bash tools/viewer.sh`.
106	
107	## What's deliberately not configured
108	
109	- No `--model` — uses the CLI's default.
110	- No assignee or label filtering. The orchestrator gates which tickets
111	  get dispatched.
112	
113	## Required setup
114	
115	In addition to `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN` (see
116	[`agents/pr-review/README.md`](../pr-review/README.md)) and the Jira
117	secrets/variables documented in
118	[`agents/jira-ticket/README.md`](../jira-ticket/README.md):
119	
120	- **`DEV_PAT`** (repo secret) — fine-grained PAT scoped to this
121	  repo with permissions: `Contents: write`, `Pull requests: write`,
122	  `Workflows: write`. Used by the agent for `git push` and
123	  `gh pr create`, and by the workflow's fallback PR step. The
124	  `Workflows: write` scope is required for tickets that scaffold a
125	  new agent (which adds a file under `.github/workflows/`); the
126	  default `GITHUB_TOKEN` cannot push workflow files. The workflow
127	  falls back to `GITHUB_TOKEN` if `DEV_PAT` is unset, but
128	  workflow-file pushes will then be rejected at push time.
129	
130	  Bonus: because the PAT is a user token rather than the runner's
131	  `GITHUB_TOKEN`, PRs it opens *do* trigger downstream workflows —
132	  so `pr-review.yml` fires automatically on junior-dev's PRs.
133	
134	- **Repo setting** — *Settings → Actions → General → Allow GitHub
135	  Actions to create and approve pull requests* must be enabled.
136	  Without this, the workflow's fallback `gh pr create` step fails
137	  with `GitHub Actions is not permitted to create or approve pull
138	  requests` even when the PAT is configured for the agent's own
139	  `gh pr create` call.
140	
141	## Known limitations
142	
143	- **No automated test step.** The agent runs whatever tests it deems
144	  relevant to the change, but there's no separate testing agent
145	  enforcing coverage. Reviewers carry that load until/unless a
146	  testing agent is added.
147

file.content:
# Junior Developer agent

Headless implementation agent for "easy" Jira tickets (story points 1–3).
Picks up a ticket key, implements the change, pushes a branch, and opens
a Draft PR. Fully autonomous — no human in the loop until the PR review.

Part of the agentic workflow sketched in [`docs/agentic-workflow.md`](../../docs/agentic-workflow.md).

## Files

- `prompt.md` — the agent's prompt. Edit this to change behavior.
- `../../.github/workflows/junior-dev.yml` — workflow that runs the agent.

## How it runs

The workflow `junior-dev.yml` triggers on `workflow_dispatch` only.
Dispatches come from two places:

- **Manual** — `gh workflow run junior-dev.yml -f ticket_key=KAN-42`.
- **Automated** — the Development Orchestrator
  ([`orchestrator.yml`](../../.github/workflows/orchestrator.yml))
  fires it on the Jira "Dev Ready" transition for every ticket with
  story points 1–3, then moves the ticket to **In Progress** so the
  same transition can't re-fire it.

The workflow installs `jira` and the Claude Code CLI, configures git,
creates branch `junior-dev/<TICKET_KEY>` from `origin/main`, then hands
off to a single `claude -p` invocation. The agent reads the ticket,
implements, commits, pushes, and opens the Draft PR itself.

## Decisions

These are the three implementation decisions KAN-2 asks the agent
author to make. Each decision and why:

- **Prompt framing — direct prompt, no skill.** `/goal` and `/ralph-loop`
  (mentioned in the original sketch) don't exist as skills in this repo,
  and the loop-control mechanism we want (`--max-turns`) is a CLI flag,
  not skill behavior. A skill would be an empty wrapper.
- **Stop condition — `--max-turns 60` + `timeout-minutes: 90`.** Two
  bounded mechanisms. `--max-turns` makes the agent fail fast on hard
  tickets without burning the full job budget. The job timeout is a
  belt-and-suspenders against any agent step that hangs (e.g. a runaway
  test invocation). An attempt counter on the ticket would race the
  orchestrator and make failures sticky across runs — rejected.
- **Branch naming — `junior-dev/<TICKET_KEY>`.** Predictable, unique
  per ticket, and namespaced so it's obvious which agent owns it.
  Re-dispatching the same ticket force-resets the branch from
  `origin/main` (workflow uses `git checkout -B`), so a re-run gets a
  clean start.

## Runtime split

This agent is the first to run **headless `claude -p` directly** rather
than through `anthropics/claude-code-action@v1`. The action stays for
read/triage agents (jira-ticket, pr-review, agent-improver) where its
event-shaped plumbing is useful. The orchestrator now runs as plain
bash with no model. For
implementation, the action's PR-comment surface is dead weight; running
the CLI directly gives full control over `--max-turns`, allowed tools,
and exit handling. See the *Design choices* section in
[`docs/agentic-workflow.md`](../../docs/agentic-workflow.md).

## Manual run

```sh
gh workflow run junior-dev.yml -f ticket_key=KAN-42
```

Optional `max_turns` input overrides `--max-turns` (default 60). Use a
small value to deliberately exercise the stop condition (see below).

## Stop-condition exercise

To see the turn cap in action against a real ticket:

```sh
gh workflow run junior-dev.yml -f ticket_key=KAN-42 -f max_turns=5
```

Expected outcome:

- Agent reads the ticket and starts implementing.
- After 5 turns, `claude` exits non-zero on `--max-turns`.
- The workflow detects the non-zero exit, still pushes whatever the
  agent committed, and the agent's pre-finish push/PR step may not
  have run — in which case the workflow opens a fallback `[WIP]` Draft
  PR titled `[WIP] [<KEY>] junior-dev hit turn cap` with a body noting
  the cap.
- Run log is committed under
  `.github/agent-logs/junior-dev/<DATE>/<RUN_ID>.json`.
- agent-improver workflow is dispatched as usual.

Record the run URL here once exercised:

> _Stop condition exercised: <run URL TBD — fill in after first invocation>_

## Logs

Each run's execution log is committed back to `main` at
`.github/agent-logs/junior-dev/<YYYY-MM-DD>/<run-id>.json`. Format
matches the array shape produced by `claude-code-action` (the workflow
runs `jq -s '.'` over the CLI's JSONL stream so the existing
agent-improver `jq` queries work unchanged). Browse with
`bash tools/viewer.sh`.

## What's deliberately not configured

- No `--model` — uses the CLI's default.
- No assignee or label filtering. The orchestrator gates which tickets
  get dispatched.

## Required setup

In addition to `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN` (see
[`agents/pr-review/README.md`](../pr-review/README.md)) and the Jira
secrets/variables documented in
[`agents/jira-ticket/README.md`](../jira-ticket/README.md):

- **`DEV_PAT`** (repo secret) — fine-grained PAT scoped to this
  repo with permissions: `Contents: write`, `Pull requests: write`,
  `Workflows: write`. Used by the agent for `git push` and
  `gh pr create`, and by the workflow's fallback PR step. The
  `Workflows: write` scope is required for tickets that scaffold a
  new agent (which adds a file under `.github/workflows/`); the
  default `GITHUB_TOKEN` cannot push workflow files. The workflow
  falls back to `GITHUB_TOKEN` if `DEV_PAT` is unset, but
  workflow-file pushes will then be rejected at push time.

  Bonus: because the PAT is a user token rather than the runner's
  `GITHUB_TOKEN`, PRs it opens *do* trigger downstream workflows —
  so `pr-review.yml` fires automatically on junior-dev's PRs.

- **Repo setting** — *Settings → Actions → General → Allow GitHub
  Actions to create and approve pull requests* must be enabled.
  Without this, the workflow's fallback `gh pr create` step fails
  with `GitHub Actions is not permitted to create or approve pull
  requests` even when the PAT is configured for the agent's own
  `gh pr create` call.

## Known limitations

- **No automated test step.** The agent runs whatever tests it deems
  relevant to the change, but there's no separate testing agent
  enforcing coverage. Reviewers carry that load until/unless a
  testing agent is added.

content.thinking:
The README is clear: `--max-turns 60` is the documented default, as stated in the Decisions section and the Manual run section. The PR is correctly aligning the workflow's default input value with what the README documents.

This is a straightforward, correct change:
- The workflow's `max_turns` input was left at `5` (a test value) which would prematurely cap all real runs.
- The README already documents `60` as the intended default.
- The fix simply updates the default from `5` to `60` and updates the description comment to match.

I don't see any bugs, security issues, or performance concerns. 

This looks good to me.

input.command: gh pr comment 54 --body "Looks good to me. The change is a straightforward alignment fix — the README and Decisions section in \`agents/junior-dev/README.md\` already document \`--max-turns 60\` as the intended default, so raising the workflow input from \`5\` to \`60\` brings the two in sync. No logic, security, or behavioural concerns."
